author: taulepton_
content:
I have been exploring how weak-subjectivity sync will work in the absence of an EL to attain full security with max efficiency. I have put together a write-up https://frisitano.github.io/slides/writeups/weak-subjectivity-checkpoint-proof-sync.html and opened a draft PR here https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5332. This proposes introducing a recursive proof layer atop execution proofs to provide CL <-> EL binding and weak-subjectivity anchoring. 

I would like to understand how other people reason about this - any feedback appreciated. Tagging a few individuals for feedback (no rush) <@427491045308235776> <@774033563732541451> <@845691100072509460> <@531934490826768418>
